ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'red wine' class (FOODON_03301826); no specific Nebbiolo Langhe DOC entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 GBK07 represents red wine generically; no Nebbiolo-specific or Langhe DOC-specific code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 is the closest parent concept for wine as a substance; no Nebbiolo-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of red wine is approximately 150 g (roughly 150 ml), equivalent to one 5-oz glass as used in clinical dietary assessments.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic table red wine used as the closest approved-source match. Nebbiolo Langhe DOC 2015 is a dry Italian red wine with a nutritional profile consistent with generic dry red wine. No HPB, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS entry exists for this specific wine. Values are representative of dry red wine at approximately 13% ABV.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC; red wine entry
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dry red wine has a reported GI of approximately 15 (glucose reference = 100) in the International GI Tables (Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr). Nebbiolo Langhe DOC is a dry red wine with minimal residual sugar (~2.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g), consistent with this GI estimate. GL per 150 g serving = (15 x 2.6 x 1.5) / 100 = 0.585, rounded to 0.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content and vasodilatory effects; low sodium
hypertension.guidance: Moderate red wine consumption (up to 1 standard drink per day) may have a mild vasodilatory effect, but regular alcohol intake can raise blood pressure over time; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to no more than one 150 ml glass per occasion and consult their physician.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and low but present carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dry red wine has a low glycemic index (~15) and low residual sugar (~0.62 g sugar per 100 g), but alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and interfere with gluconeogenesis; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume no more than one standard drink with a meal and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Polyphenol content (resveratrol, anthocyanins) versus alcohol-related cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Nebbiolo-based red wines are rich in polyphenols including resveratrol and anthocyanins, which have been associated with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects in observational studies; however, current cardiovascular guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardioprotection, and individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit intake to no more than one standard drink per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nebbiolo Langhe DOC 2015 by Giacomo Fenocchio is a dry Italian red wine produced from 100% Nebbiolo grapes grown in the Langhe appellation of Piemonte, northwestern Italy. The Langhe DOC designation is a controlled appellation of origin (Denominazione di Origine Controllata) that permits Nebbiolo wines of high quality from this historic wine-producing region. Giacomo Fenocchio is a renowned family estate based in Monforte d'Alba, Piemonte, known for traditional winemaking methods. The 2015 vintage is considered an exceptional year in Piemonte due to warm, dry growing conditions that produced concentrated, well-structured wines. The wine presents a deep ruby-red colour with garnet reflections, an intense and fruity bouquet of plum, cherry, dried rose, and subtle earthy notes characteristic of the Nebbiolo grape. On the palate it is dry, full-bodied, with firm tannins, good acidity, and a long finish. Nutritionally, dry red wine provides approximately 85 kcal per 100 g, with minimal protein (0.07 g), negligible fat (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central), and low carbohydrate (2.6 g) predominantly as residual sugar. It contains polyphenols including resveratrol and anthocyanins. The glycemic index is low (~15). Clinically, alcohol content warrants caution for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. A standard serving is 150 ml (approximately 150 g). This wine is not halal and is not suitable for individuals who abstain from alcohol.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in Nebbiolo Langhe DOC wine?
Is Nebbiolo wine good for diabetics?
Langhe DOC red wine glycemic index

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy generic dry red wine (FDC ID 174837), which is the closest approved-source match. No specific entry for Nebbiolo Langhe DOC or Giacomo Fenocchio 2015 exists in any approved primary nutrient database. The adaptation is defensible given that Nebbiolo Langhe DOC is a dry red wine with ABV and residual sugar consistent with the USDA reference. GI is estimated from peer-reviewed International GI Tables for dry red wine.
notes: 1. fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for table red wine (FDC ID 174837); naturally zero as wine contains no dietary fat by composition. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero per the same source. 2. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as wine is a liquid fermented beverage with no intact plant fibre. 3. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero; naturally zero as wine is a plant-derived fermented beverage. 4. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; destroyed during fermentation. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as wine is not an animal product. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero. 8. vitamin_e_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 9. The input food_group was listed as 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect; corrected to 'Beverages' as this is a wine. 10. This wine is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk restaurant in Singapore via GrabFood at SGD 129.58 per bottle. 11. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian wine product; no Singaporean-cuisine multilingual requirement applies as this is an Italian beverage, not a Singaporean dish. 12. glycemic_index set to 15 based on International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2008) for dry red wine; gi_category is low (GI <= 55). GL per 150 g serving = (15 x 2.6 x 1.5) / 100 = 0.585, rounded to 0.6. 13. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central, not -1) as the source explicitly reports these as zero for table red wine.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name for this specific wine product. Cuisine is Italian, not Singaporean, so multilingual local-language name requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply under G6. The romanized_local alias is provided.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (85.0), protein_g (0.07), fat_g (0.0), and carbohydrate_g (2.6) are all present and numeric. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174837 (table red wine), an approved secondary global source. fat_g = 0.0 is justified as confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and naturally zero for wine.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (4.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), and sugar_g (0.62) are all present as numeric values, plausible for dry red wine, and supported by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index = 15 (numeric, > 0), gi_category = 'low' (GI 15 <= 55, correct threshold), glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.6, gi_source present. GI sourced from International GI Tables (Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr), a peer-reviewed journal, as analogous estimate for dry red wine.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301826 (red wine, closest_parent), FoodEx2 GBK07 (Wine, red, closest_parent), SNOMED CT 35748005 (Wine substance, closest_parent) are all defensible real codes. No codes are invented. Gap notes explain why exact matches are unavailable.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, production method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. Includes polyphenol content, GI, serving size, and clinical cautions.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct, machine key type2_diabetes), Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 9 entries covering alternate English names, producer name variants, appellation variants, and regional descriptors. aliases_by_language is populated with English and romanized entries. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. No mandatory multilingual aliases required for Italian cuisine under G6/Q8 rules.

Added zero-macro justifications for fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, and other confirmed-zero nutrients. Set GI to 15 (numeric, > 0) with gi_category 'low'. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Ensured all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Documented all sentinel and zero values in notes.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — All six hard gates pass. Score 8/8. Nebbiolo Langhe DOC 2015 by Giacomo Fenocchio correctly classified as a dry Italian red wine in the Beverages food group. Nutrients adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (approved secondary source) for generic table red wine, with adaptation documented. GI of 15 sourced from peer-reviewed International GI Tables for dry red wine. Ontology anchors use real codes at closest_parent level with honest gap notes. No Singaporean-cuisine multilingual requirements apply. All zero and sentinel values documented. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance.
